R302.3.description: Minimum clear width of pedestrian access route
R302.3.notes: Continuous clear width of 4 ft minimum; 5 ft passing spaces required at intervals no greater than 200 ft

R302.5.description: Maximum running slope of pedestrian access route
R302.5.notes: Running slope shall not exceed 5% (1:20); steeper slopes require ramp standards

R302.6.description: Maximum cross slope of pedestrian access route
R302.6.notes: Cross slope shall not exceed 2% (1:48) at pedestrian access routes

R302.7.description: Surface requirements for pedestrian access route
R302.7.notes: Surfaces shall be firm, stable, and slip resistant

R305.1.description: Curb ramp requirements at pedestrian crossings
R305.1.notes: Curb ramps required wherever pedestrian access route crosses a curb

R305.2.notes: Clear width of curb ramp run shall be 4 ft minimum, exclusive of flared sides. This is a ramp design requirement, not a cross-section curb element width.

R306.2.description: Accessible pedestrian signal push button location
R306.2.notes: Push buttons shall be located adjacent to a level clear space and within reach range

R402.1.description: Pedestrian access route clear width in medians and islands
R402.1.notes: Pedestrian access routes through medians and pedestrian refuge islands shall be 4 ft minimum clear width

R210.1.description: Furniture and objects shall not reduce accessible path width
R210.1.notes: Street furniture, signs, and other objects shall not reduce the clear width of the pedestrian access route below 4 ft

R302.3.1.description: Passing spaces on pedestrian access route
R302.3.1.notes: Where continuous clear width is less than 5 ft, passing spaces of 5 ft x 5 ft shall be provided at intervals no greater than 200 ft
